message:
fix(generators): fast_gnp_random_graph silent-clamp p outside [0,1] (br-r37-c1-lsdek)

networkx.fast_gnp_random_graph silently treats p<=0 as empty and p>=1
as complete (matching gnp_random_graph). fnx routed all create_using=None
calls through the Rust _rust_fast_gnp fast path, which raises
ValueError: FailClosed { operation: "fast_gnp_random_graph", reason: "p=..." }
for any out-of-range p — breaking drop-in calls like
fast_gnp_random_graph(5, 1.0) which nx returns as K5.

Fix: only enter the Rust fast path when 0 < p < 1; otherwise delegate
to gnp_random_graph (which already handles the boundary correctly).
gnp_random_graph itself was already fine — the bead title mentioned
both, but only the fast variant had the regression.

Verified:
- fast_gnp_random_graph(5, -1, seed=42) → 0 edges (was: ValueError)
- fast_gnp_random_graph(5, 0, seed=42) → 0 edges
- fast_gnp_random_graph(5, 1, seed=42) → 10 edges (K5)
- fast_gnp_random_graph(5, 2, seed=42) → 10 edges (was: ValueError)
- 170 OrangeHorse smoke tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34003,14 +34003,20 @@ def fast_gnp_random_graph(n, p, seed=None, directed=False, *, create_using=None,
     if n < 0:
         raise NetworkXError(f"Negative number of nodes not valid: {n}")
 
-    if create_using is None:
+    # br-r37-c1-lsdek: out-of-range p is silent in nx (p<=0 -> empty,
+    # p>=1 -> complete). The Rust fast path raises FailClosed for those,
+    # so route to the boundary-aware gnp_random_graph wrapper.
+    if create_using is None and 0 < p < 1:
         return _rust_fast_gnp(
             n,
             p,
             seed=_native_random_seed(seed),
             directed=directed,
         )
 
+    if create_using is None and not (0 < p < 1):
+        return gnp_random_graph(n, p, seed=seed, directed=directed)
+
     rng = _generator_random_state(seed)
     default = DiGraph if directed else Graph
     graph = _checked_create_using(
